metrics.label: Bridges in poor condition
metrics.source: FHWA National Bridge Inventory / ARTBA Bridge Report
metrics.note: Indiana statewide is 5.3% structurally deficient; Allen County appears in the top-traveled deficient list but county aggregate not published, so modeled at roughly the state rate.

metrics.label: Roads in poor condition
metrics.note: Modeled from Indiana urban-area ride-quality norms; no county-specific HPMS share retrieved.

metrics.label: Roundabout ratio
metrics.note: 58 roundabout ways vs 777 signalized-intersection nodes in OpenStreetMap; reported as an adoption index (shrunk toward zero for low counts).

metrics.label: Traffic deaths
metrics.note: Allen County recorded 35 motor-vehicle deaths in 2024 (~9.0/100k on ~390k pop); modeled at ~10.5/100k to reflect a multi-year average, below the Indiana county median of ~15.8.

metrics.label: Sidewalk coverage
metrics.note: Modeled/partial: dense sidewalk grid in Fort Wayne's urban core plus 160+ miles of trails, but sparse in suburban and rural Allen County.

metrics.label: Park acres per 1,000 residents
metrics.source: Fort Wayne Parks & Recreation / Trust for Public Land
metrics.note: Fort Wayne Parks maintains ~2,840 acres across ~89 parks; adding Allen County / New Haven parkland against a ~390k county population yields roughly 10-11 acres/1k.

metrics.label: Residents within a 10-min walk of a park
metrics.note: Modeled from TPL ParkScore norms for mid-size Midwestern cities; strong core coverage, lower in outlying county.

metrics.label: Playground quality
metrics.note: Fort Wayne has recently added spraygrounds and modern playgrounds (Buckner, Robert E. Meyers Park at Harrison Square); modeled above average given active capital reinvestment.

metrics.label: Library visits per capita
metrics.note: Allen County Public Library recorded 1,812,529 in-person visits against a legal service population of 385,410 (~4.7 per capita); ranks #2 in Indiana by annual visits.

metrics.label: Tree canopy
metrics.source: Indiana DNR Urban Tree Canopy / USFS
metrics.note: Fort Wayne's tree canopy declined from 29% to 23% over the last decade (urban development, storms, emerald ash borer); parks dept actively replanting.

metrics.label: Learning rate
metrics.note: Allen County spans multiple districts; suburban NACS/SACS post strong gains while Fort Wayne Community Schools recover post-pandemic (leading state in reading growth). County-wide modeled near the national ~1.0 grade/yr.

metrics.label: Grade levels vs. national average
metrics.note: Modeled slightly below the national mean, reflecting a mix of high-performing suburban districts and a large urban district with lower proficiency (FWCS math/reading ~50-60% proficient).

metrics.label: Bachelor's degree or higher (age 25+)
metrics.note: ACS 2023 1-year: 32.3% of adults 25+ hold a bachelor's degree or higher.

metrics.label: Life expectancy
metrics.note: Allen County life expectancy 75.6 years per County Health Rankings 2025.

metrics.label: Child wellbeing index
metrics.source: modeled from CDC PLACES / KIDS COUNT
metrics.note: Modeled composite; Allen County carries elevated adult obesity (38%), smoking (19%), and overdose (34.8/100k) that weigh on child outcomes, offset by strong library/parks access.

metrics.label: Violent crime
metrics.note: Fort Wayne reported 732 violent crimes in 2023, ~271/100k = ~2.7/1k; county-wide rate is somewhat lower given lower-crime suburban/rural areas.

metrics.label: Drinking-water violation severity
metrics.note: Fort Wayne City Utilities is a large surface-water system; a Lead and Copper Rule action-level exceedance has been reported, otherwise generally compliant with no detected PFAS. Modeled at 2 (minor).

metrics.label: Broadband 100/20 Mbps availability
metrics.note: Xfinity cable reaches ~99% of Fort Wayne; county-wide 100/20 availability modeled at ~97% accounting for rural gaps.

metrics.label: Gigabit availability
metrics.note: Frontier fiber (up to 7 Gbps) and Xfinity gigabit cover most of Fort Wayne; modeled at ~85% county-wide with lower rural fiber reach.

metrics.label: Historic Register listings per 10k
metrics.note: 79 NRHP properties/districts in Allen County (incl. 2 National Historic Landmarks) against ~390k pop = ~2.0 per 10k.

metrics.label: Cultural venues per 10k
metrics.note: Fort Wayne has an unusually deep arts core (Embassy Theatre, Philharmonic, Ballet, Civic Theatre, Museum of Art, Arts Campus, Science Central, plus ~10 museums); ~18-20 venues / 39 (per-10k units) ~ 0.5.

metrics.label: Town center quality
metrics.note: Downtown Fort Wayne is a genuinely walkable, actively revitalized center: The Landing, Promenade Park, Parkview Field/Harrison Square, Electric Works, DORA district, Urban Trail, Arts Campus.

metrics.label: Capital outlay per capita per year
metrics.source: Census of Governments / Fort Wayne ACFR
metrics.note: Modeled from Fort Wayne's sustained capital program (riverfront, streets, parks) plus ~$1B downtown investment over a decade; above typical for a modest-income Midwestern city.

metrics.label: Net capital assets per capita
metrics.note: Modeled net governmental capital assets per resident; exact ACFR figure not retrieved (PDF unavailable), estimated from comparable mid-size city balance sheets and Fort Wayne's large recent build-out.

metrics.label: Investment trend
metrics.note: Very high and rising: ~$1B downtown investment over the decade, $20M Promenade Park, $89M Riverfront at Promenade Park, $28.3M Riverfront Phase IIB (2026), $286-440M Electric Works, Harrison Square/Parkview Field, and a new 10-year downtown plan. A place actively investing in its public realm now.
